title: Hardware Technician
description: A Hardware Technician in India installs, configures, and troubleshoots computer hardware, peripherals, and networking equipment. They work with clients to identify hardware issues, repair or replace faulty components, and ensure smooth system operation. Their daily tasks involve hands-on technical work, communication with clients, and staying updated with the latest hardware technologies.
entrance_exams: DTE, Maharashtra State Board of Technical Education, AICTE
degree_name: Diploma in Computer Hardware and Networking
top_private_colleges: Fr. Conceicao Rodrigues College of Engineering, Mumbai
Sardar Patel College of Engineering, Mumbai

career_progression.year_0_2: Assist senior technicians, learn hardware installation and configuration, and develop problem-solving skills.
career_progression.year_3_5: Work independently on hardware repairs, upgrade systems, and provide technical support to clients.
career_progression.year_6_10: Take on leadership roles, manage teams, and oversee large-scale hardware projects.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a hardware repair and maintenance business, or create and sell hardware-related products.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities exist in the Middle East, Europe, and the US, particularly in the IT and electronics sectors.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Initial years may involve working long hours, including evenings and weekends, to meet client demands.
common_misconceptions: Hardware technicians only work with computers.
Hardware technicians do not require any formal education.
